Condition-Based Maintenance at Scale

Part 2: Managing People and Change

Home Industries Proof Resources Contact Assets

The Human Factor

Technology is only half the story. Successful condition-based maintenance transformation requires engagement, training, and change management.

Stakeholder Engagement

Secure buy-in from multiple levels:

  • Executive leadership for strategic alignment and budget
  • Operations managers who will implement daily processes
  • Field technicians who execute maintenance work
  • IT teams who manage systems and data

Training and Development

Invest in comprehensive training programs covering the new technologies, processes, and interpretation of condition monitoring data. Continuous learning is essential as people adapt to new ways of working.

Change Management

Acknowledge that condition-based maintenance represents a fundamental shift from time-based thinking. Effective communication about benefits, clear transition plans, and support for resistant stakeholders are critical.

Next: Process

In Part 3, we'll explore the process changes required to fully leverage condition-based maintenance.
